up.method: TLC; L. symbiosum reduced inulin to a lesser extent than P. dorei (partial fructan utilization)

sec.method: quantified in monoculture supernatant after 48 h; 20-30 mM detected across all carbon sources and control
L. symbiosum monoculture supernatants contained no lactate in any sample

growth_records.cite: Hirmas et al. Metabolic Modeling and Bidirectional Culturing of Two Gut Microbes Reveal Cross-Feeding Interactions and Protective Effects on Intestinal Cells. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00646-22. [PMC9600892]
growth_records.snippet: concentrations between 20 and 30 mM butyrate were detected in L. symbiosum monocultures in the tested carbon sources and control medium ... L. symbiosum monoculture supernatants did not contain lactate in any sample.
growth_records.notes: L. symbiosum monoculture on 2% inulin (mZMB, 37C, anaerobic, 48 h): butyrate 20-30 mM (experimental endpoint; substrate-independent, also present in no-carbon control, so partly amino-acid fermentation). No lactate produced. Partial fructan utilization. Values are endpoint concentrations (mM), not per-biomass rates.

up.method: TLC; L. symbiosum was unable to degrade xylan

sec.method: quantified in monoculture supernatant after 48 h; 20-30 mM detected across all carbon sources and control
L. symbiosum monoculture supernatants contained no lactate in any sample

growth_records.cite: Hirmas et al. Metabolic Modeling and Bidirectional Culturing of Two Gut Microbes Reveal Cross-Feeding Interactions and Protective Effects on Intestinal Cells. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00646-22. [PMC9600892]
growth_records.snippet: L. symbiosum was unable to degrade xylan ... concentrations between 20 and 30 mM butyrate were detected in L. symbiosum monocultures in the tested carbon sources and control medium.
growth_records.notes: L. symbiosum monoculture on 2% xylan (mZMB, 37C, anaerobic, 48 h): cannot degrade xylan (zero xylan uptake). Butyrate 20-30 mM (substrate-independent basal butyrogenesis, present even in no-carbon control - amino-acid fermentation). No lactate. Endpoint concentrations, not per-biomass rates.
